Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The main flow appears to be: Receive Application (RA) -> Validate Form (VF) -> Assess Eligibility (AE) -> Approval Decision (AD) -> Send Confirmation (SC) -> Archive Case (AC). However, there is also an optional loop: Request Missing Info (RMI) and then re-Validate Form (VF). This loop can happen multiple times (case 3 has two RMI and two VF after the first VF).

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets examine the traces. Case 1: RA -> VF -> AE -> RMI -> VF -> AD -> SC -> AC. So after AE, there is RMI, then VF, then AD. Case 3: RA -> VF -> AE -> RMI -> RMI -> VF -> AD -> SC -> AC.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - NE
Text: the loop could be: after AE, you have a XOR choice: either go to AD, or go into a loop that does: (RMI)*, then VF, then back to the choice.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: the loop operator in process trees is defined as: * ( A, B ) means A is executed, then either exit the loop, or execute B and then A again. So the possible traces are A, or A B A, or A B A B A, etc.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - NE
Text: set A = -> (RMI, VF) and B = AD? But then the loop would be: first execute RMI VF, then either exit or execute AD and then RMI VF again.

Name: Validation - NE
Text: That would produce: either just RMI VF, or RMI VF AD RMI VF, etc. But that would allow AD to be inside the loop, which is not what we want.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: We want the loop to be optional (zero occurrences). The * operator always executes A at least once, so it cannot represent zero occurrences. However, we can combine with XOR to make it optional: XOR( tau, * (A, B) ).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: after AE, we have a XOR between tau (skip) and * (RMI, tau), then VF, then AD.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: -> ( X ( tau, -> ( * ( RMI, tau ), VF ) ), AD ).

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If we choose tau: AE tau AD -> AE AD. If choose sequence: * (RMI, tau) then VF then AD. For one RMI: exit after first RMI, get RMI VF AD. For two: RMI tau RMI VF AD -> RMI RMI VF AD.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: balances fitness, precision, and simplicity, capturing these possible repetitions of requesting missing info and re-validation.
